headline: Can I Just Replace the Door or Do I Need a Whole New Frame?
articleBody:
That's one of the first questions people ask when thinking about a new door. The answer really depends on the condition of your existing door frame. The two main options you'll hear about are a 'slab' door and a 'pre-hung' door.

A 'slab' is just the door itself. You can go this route if your current frame is perfectly square, plumb, and level, with no signs of damage or rot. A slab installation requires precise work to mortise hinges and drill for the lockset to match the existing frame perfectly. A 'pre-hung' door, on the other hand, comes as a complete package—the door is already hanging in its own new frame. This is almost always the better choice, especially for exterior doors where a tight seal is critical.

So, when is a new frame necessary? If you can see rot at the bottom of the jambs, if the frame is warped, or if the opening has settled out of square, a pre-hung unit is the way to go. It ensures the door and frame are perfectly matched for a weathertight seal and smooth operation from day one. It addresses any underlying issues with the opening instead of just covering them up. Getting a professional opinion is key here; a quick inspection can tell you whether a simple slab swap is realistic or if a full pre-hung system is needed for a lasting, quality result. If you're in the Bay Area, we'd be happy to take a look and advise on the best approach.
